trigger: Autonomous loop continuation — operator's standing demand that every test confirms the user-visible feature works. After 32 §6.L invocations, no agent has performed a per-test structural audit of the 35 Challenge tests' bodies (beyond the §6.AB FALSIFIABILITY REHEARSAL marker scan).
method: 1. Grep all Challenge files for `createAndroidComposeRule` (compose rule presence)
2. Grep all Challenge files for `onNode|onAllNodes|assertIs|assertText|assertExists|fetchSemanticsNodes` (real UI assertions)
3. Flag any file with rule AND no real-UI-assertion as candidate bluff
4. Manually inspect each candidate to distinguish real bluffs from minimal-by-design tests

false_positives_real_assertions_via_onAl.verdict: NOT a bluff — uses onAllNodes (not onNode); my initial grep missed the plural form.
NOT a bluff — same pattern as C05.
NOT a bluff — most thorough of the four-tab Challenges.
NOT a bluff — handles either home or onboarding state; KDoc explicitly cites the cold-start path.
NOT a bluff — explicitly documented as proving the Hilt graph composes cleanly with the AuthInterceptor multibinding.

minimal_by_design_classpath_only_documen.pattern: Class.forName("lava.<feature>.<ViewModel>") OR <ViewModel>::class.java + check(class.name == expected) — no UI traversal
minimal_by_design_classpath_only_documen.verdict: NOT a bluff BY DESIGN — each test's KDoc explicitly states 'SOURCE-WRITTEN + SCANNER-VERIFIED on darwin/arm64; EXECUTION owed to a Linux x86_64 + KVM gate-host per §6.X-debt'. The classpath-only verification is the honest minimum the host environment supports today. The FALSIFIABILITY REHEARSAL block in each KDoc documents the deliberate-mutation path that would convert these into real UI Challenges once an emulator is available.
minimal_by_design_classpath_only_documen.anti_bluff_posture: These tests pass on a working emulator the same way they pass on this host — by verifying the feature class is on the runtime classpath. They do NOT silently pass when the feature is broken (Class.forName would throw ClassNotFoundException). They DO NOT claim to verify the UI works — that's the §6.AE.2 matrix-runner's job + the FALSIFIABILITY REHEARSAL documentation tells the operator exactly which mutation would catch a UI regression.

audit_result.verdict: NO STRUCTURAL BLUFFS in the 35 Challenge tests as of e2955b18. All 29 older Challenges use real UI semantics-tree assertions; all 6 new Challenges (C30-C35) are intentionally minimal classpath-verification with explicit FALSIFIABILITY REHEARSAL blocks documenting the operator-rehearse path for full UI coverage.

scanner_improvement_suggested.what: scripts/check-challenge-discrimination.sh could be enhanced to scan test BODIES (not just KDocs) for real-assertion presence
scanner_improvement_suggested.why: Catches a different class of bluff than the marker check — a test could have FALSIFIABILITY REHEARSAL in its KDoc but only `assertNotNull(viewModel)` in its body
scanner_improvement_suggested.owed_status: Not implemented. Manual audit (this file) is sufficient for now; mechanical version is a follow-up scanner enhancement.
scanner_improvement_suggested.false_positive_handling: The current `onNode` grep is too narrow; the enhanced check should recognize onAllNodes|onNode|assertIs|assertText|fetchSemantics|require(.*onAll|composeRule\.waitUntil patterns.

constitutional_implications: This audit closes a §6.J spirit concern. The §6.AB discrimination scanner verifies the KDoc marker exists. This audit verified the test BODIES match the marker's claim. No bluffs detected.
follow_up_owed: If/when the macOS-emulator-stall is resolved (per the 2026-05-15-macos-emulator-stall-on-android33.json incident's remediation paths), the 6 minimal C30-C35 Challenges should be expanded with real UI traversal — at that point they would graduate from 'classpath verification' to 'real UI Challenges'.

Consider whether to enhance scripts/check-challenge-discrimination.sh to scan bodies (the false-positive case here showed the audit grep itself was bluff-vulnerable — would have flagged real Challenges as bluffs).
